expectation: High autonomy should not result in AI-dominated or weak regulation.
rationale: High system autonomy increases the chance that regulation shifts away from learner and teacher.
Proactive autonomy in instruction or later phases requires human-in-the-loop supervision.
High autonomy becomes blocking only when it enters learning-facing phases without the required supervision.
Adaptive processing is didactically safer when learner, teacher and system all keep a regulative role.
effect: Pedagogical adaptivity is strongest when triadic co-regulation remains intact.

expectation: VM >= VM3; Ad5 + VM4 produces synergy
on_violation: warn: technical adaptivity may displace metacognition
rationale: Adaptivity without metacognitive support optimizes output, not learning.

expectation: Conceptual and metacognitive knowledge work should use at least monitored and preferably corrected bias handling.
rationale: Bias is more harmful when the system shapes concepts, interpretations or metacognitive beliefs.
Manifest or subtle bias is unacceptable in practice, assessment or other consequential learning phases.

trigger: C = C1 OR (A >= A4 AND C <= C2)
expectation: Regulation should remain distributed when autonomy is high.
rationale: AI-dominated regulation becomes more risky as system autonomy rises.
Triadic co-regulation cannot be claimed when the interaction is fully or almost fully individual.
requirement: C5 requires a social or teacher-involved setting; normally S >= S3, preferably S4

expectation: VM >= VM2; C5 + VM4 + X4 produces high-level SRL synergy
rationale: Shared regulation and metacognitive support reinforce each other.

expectation: Visible or deep reasoning should have at least partial epistemic anchoring.
rationale: Reasoning depth without epistemic anchoring can create convincing but unreliable output.

expectation: Meta-deliberative systems should also support learner metacognition.
rationale: System self-reflection can replace rather than develop learner reflection.
Internal deliberation without traceability breaks didactic controllability.
Corrective supervision must rise with autonomous initiative in learning-facing phases.
Practice and assessment phases require stronger supervision than preparatory use.
requirement: DBc >= DBc3; if P >= P5 then DBc >= DBc4

on_violation: downgrade DBc confidence or raise oversight_trace_gap
rationale: Supervision cannot be verified when process information is not inspectable.
requirement: Contingent or active supervision needs enough trace to inspect triggers, decisions and corrections.

rationale: High epistemic reliability requires visible or reconstructable reasoning, not only polished output.
requirement: DA >= DA2 for reconstructable reasoning; E4 + DA2 produces synergy

rationale: Verifiability requires traceable inputs, claims or transformations.

expectation: Conceptual, metacognitive or integrated knowledge should be at least qualified and epistemically anchored.
rationale: Higher-order knowledge work is fragile when claims are weakly anchored.

on_violation: no_violation; add trace note when K and P appear mismatched
rationale: The same process phase has a different risk profile for factual, procedural, conceptual and metacognitive work.
effect: Use knowledge type to interpret whether a process phase is pedagogically fitting.

trigger: K >= K3 AND VC < VC3 AND VM < VM2
expectation: Higher-order knowledge should have matching cognitive or metacognitive support.
rationale: Knowledge type sets the kind of support needed for effective learning.

expectation: High task transfer should not be combined with low continuity if learning claims are made.
rationale: The learner can appear successful during AI-supported work without durable transfer.

expectation: Transfer and self-generated continuity normally require systematic metacognitive support.
rationale: Durable learning over time depends on planning, monitoring and reflection.

expectation: High autonomy in instruction or later phases requires explicit supervision and accountability.
on_violation: warn; if DBc != DBc5 then HC3 invalidates configuration
rationale: Autonomy risk grows sharply once the system participates in teaching, practice or assessment.
Bias cannot be accepted in phases with direct practice or assessment consequences.
Later process phases need stronger correction and supervision.
requirement: DBc >= DBc3; if P >= P5 then DBc >= DBc4

expectation: Practice and assessment phases should provide full traceability or better.
rationale: Higher-stakes phases require inspectable process evidence even when not listed as a hard invalidation.
C5 depends on the presence of a real social or teacher-mediated learning relation.
requirement: Triadic co-regulation requires enough social or teacher-involved structure.

expectation: VS >= VS2; VS4 strengthens socially shared metacognition
on_violation: warn: social setting lacks skill support
rationale: Social learning works better when the system actively supports social learning skills.

expectation: High task transfer should be tested for durable transfer.
rationale: Performance under AI control is not evidence of learning continuity.
The same level of task transfer has different meaning in preparation, instruction, practice and assessment.
effect: Weight TD risk more heavily in P4-P5 than in P1-P2.

rationale: If the system performs the core skill, it cannot also count as supporting the learner on that same skill.
requirement: VC <= VC2 OR VM <= VM2 on the same skill x

trigger: T >= T4 AND A >= A4 AND (DBc < DBc4 OR X < X3)
expectation: Multi-system integration with high autonomy needs active supervision and traceability.
rationale: Integrated autonomous systems create responsibility and accountability gaps.

trigger: T >= T2 AND X < X2 OR T >= T4 AND X < X4
expectation: Higher technical integration needs stronger transparency.
rationale: The more systems exchange data or coordinate, the more inspectability is needed.

expectation: Affective support should not combine with AI-dominated regulation.
rationale: Strong affective modulation is safer when regulation remains shared.

expectation: Conceptual or integrated knowledge requires at least structured cognitive support.
rationale: Higher-order knowledge needs more than retrieval-level support.

trigger: TD >= TD4 AND VC >= VC3 on the same skill x
rationale: Skill support and skill takeover cannot be credited on the same learner skill.
requirement: Do not count AI task takeover as cognitive skill support for the learner.

expectation: VM >= VM3; Ad5 + VM4 produces synergy
on_violation: warn: adaptivity may bypass learner metacognition
rationale: Metacognitive support turns technical adaptivity into pedagogical adaptivity.

expectation: VM >= VM2; C5 + VM4 + X4 is optimal for SRL
on_violation: warn: shared regulation lacks metacognitive backbone
rationale: Co-regulation and metacognition reinforce each other.

expectation: Transfer-level continuity requires systematic metacognitive support.
rationale: Continuity over contexts needs learner planning, monitoring and reflection.

expectation: Social setting and social skill support should be aligned.
on_violation: warn: social environment and social support are mismatched
rationale: A social learning space needs corresponding skill support, and vice versa.
Deliberation hidden from inspection is not didactically controllable.

expectation: Higher-stakes learning phases require full traceability or better.
rationale: Practice and assessment need process evidence for correction and accountability.

trigger: T >= T2 AND X < X2 OR T >= T4 AND X < X4
expectation: Technical integration level and transparency level should rise together.
rationale: Integrated systems need inspectable data flows, decisions and corrections.
